data.title: Wait on Rivian's Gen 3 Platform Could Hurt Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Rivian not introducing its Gen 3 platform until early 2027 poses a significant risk for near-term demand, say Morgan Stanley analysts. Autonomous optionality is core value proposition of battery electric vehicles and the delay of the most advanced autonomy platform could hurt customer interest for now, the analysts say. "The decision to tier the hardware sensor suite introduces an 'upper-bound' to autonomous capabilities on earlier generation vehicles…potentially driving more customers to wait for the next gen product," they say. That could hurt demand for both the company's R1 and R2 cars, extending the path to profitability.(nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Venezuela's oil exports jump to 1.23 million bpd with more sales to US, India
data.description: Venezuela's oil exports rose 14% to 1.23 million barrels per day (bpd) in April, fueled by more sales to the United States, India and Europe, shipping data and documents from state company PDVSA showed on Friday.

data.title: Bank of New York Mellon Corp 1Q 2026: Revenue $5.41B, EPS $2.24— 10-Q Summary
data.description:
Bank of New York Mellon Corp reported first-quarter 2026 results with revenue of $5.41B and diluted EPS of $2.24, reflecting year‑over‑year growth driven by core product lines and recurring services.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue was $5.41B, compared with $4.79B in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 12.9%.
  • Net income was $1.63B, compared with $1.22B in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 33.8%.
  • Diluted EPS was $2.24, compared with $1.58 in the prior-year quarter; YoY change 41.8%.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth was driven by core product lines and recurring services, supporting sequential expansion.
  • Shift toward digital and direct-to-consumer channels continued, reducing reliance on third-party distributors.
  • Brand momentum improved through new product launches and marketing efforts, boosting engagement and retention.
  • Operational initiatives completed included capacity upgrades and supply-chain resiliency measures, shortening lead times and improving fulfillment.

Original SEC Filing: Bank of New York Mellon Corp [ BK ] - 10-Q - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: ICE Canada Morning Comment: Increases in Canola Amid Choppy Activity
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange turned higher in choppy trading Friday morning.

Canola gleaned support from gains in the Chicago soy complex, but declines in European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil limited its upside.

Crude oil turned lower on news Iran sent a new set of proposals to the United States in hopes of restarting talks.

The July canola contract remained above its major technical levels, further underpinning the oilseed.

Canola crush margins saw some contraction in the July position, with it slipping to around C$347 per tonne above the futures.

The new crop November positions nudged up, with that for October delivery just over C$292 per tonne above the futures and December about C$283.

Statistics Canada is scheduled to release its stocks of principal field crops report Wednesday. It's widely expected the report will have little effect on canola futures.

The Canadian dollar was stronger on Friday morning at 73.77 U.S. cents compared with Thursday's close of 73.40.

About 9,150 contracts had been traded by 9:45 a.m. ET and prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne were:

Canola 
           Price      Change 
Jul       767.00     up 3.20 
Nov       764.50     up 3.20 
Jan       770.20     up 3.10 
Mar       774.30     up 2.80

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com

data.title: TMC says seabed mining application meets US norms, sees regulatory decision by Q1 2027
data.description:
The Metals Co said on Friday U.S. regulators have found its consolidated application for deep seabed mineral exploration and commercial recovery to be fully compliant with federal requirements, marking a key step toward potential approval.

The company said it expects the regulatory process, including environmental review, to conclude by the end of first-quarter 2027.

The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration determined that the application submitted by its U.S. unit meets all requirements under the Deep Seabed Hard Mineral Resources Act, TMC said.

The decision comes as President Donald Trump's administration encourages U.S. exploration of the deep sea by accelerating permits for companies seeking critical minerals in international waters, a move expected to face environmental and legal challenges.

Interest in mining the world's seabeds has grown amid efforts to secure supplies of nickel, manganese, copper and other metals used in electronics, weapons and consumer goods, though the practice has been opposed by environmental groups.

TMC has sought approval to explore and recover polymetallic nodules containing metals used in energy, defense and infrastructure applications. In January, the company said it became the first deep-sea miner to seek Washington's approval under a streamlined permitting process introduced earlier this year.

data.title: Occidental Petroleum Names a New CEO. How the Stock Has Fared Under Vicki Hollub. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kit Norton

The Vicki Hollub era at Occidental Petroleum is coming to an end, the Houston-based energy company announced Friday.

Richard Jackson, senior vice president and chief operating officer, will take over as CEO of the oil-and-gas exploration company from Hollub upon her retirement on June 1, according to Occidental Petroleum.

Jackson joined Occidental in 2003 in its Middle East operations and has since risen through the ranks. He replaces Hollub, who has served as Occidental CEO since April 2016.

Occidental Petroleum shares declined 3.3% to $55.59 on Friday after closing down 0.3% on Thursday.

Reports emerged in March that Occidental planned to name Jackson as the next CEO.

Hollub, 66 years old, oversaw a period of change at the U.S. energy company. That included the $55 billion purchase of Anadarko Petroleum in 2019 that substantially increased Occidental's U.S. production, boosting its exposure in the desirable Permian basin region of Texas.

However, Hollub's time leading the company has been marked by her willingness to use debt to make acquisitions, and then the need to reduce that debt to clean up the balance sheet.

Hollub started working at Occidental Petroleum 1982. After retirement, Hollub will remain on the board of directors, Occidental said.

Occidental Petroleum stock hasn't performed well under Hollub. Since she became CEO in April 2016, shares have fallen 21%, according to Dow Jones Market Data. For comparison, supermajors Chevron and Exxon Mobil have gained 89% and 75%, respectively, in that time frame.

So far this year, Occidental Petroleum stock has rallied 47%, boosted by higher oil prices due to the U.S. war with Iran.

"Following the recently completed decade-long transformation of the company, we now have the best portfolio and the best technical expertise in Occidental's history. With this strong foundation in place, a clear path forward and a leader like Richard, who has the experience and vision to elevate Occidental, now is the right time for this transition, " Hollub said in the press release.

Write to Kit Norton at kit.norton@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Aon reports Q1 2026: Revenue $5.03B, Operating income $1.72B, Adj. diluted EPS $6.48
data.description:
Aon delivered a strong first quarter of 2026 with total revenue of $5.03 billion, operating income of $1.72 billion and adjusted diluted EPS of $6.48. The company reported 6% total revenue growth and 5% organic revenue growth versus the prior year, generated $363 million of free cash flow and returned $662 million to shareholders through dividends and repurchases. Management reaffirmed 2026 targets including mid-single-digit or greater organic revenue growth and continued adjusted operating margin expansion.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $5,034 million for Q1 2026, up 6% from $4,729 million in Q1 2025.
  • Operating income: $1,715 million for Q1 2026, up 17% from $1,461 million in Q1 2025; operating margin 34.1%.
  • Adjusted operating income: $1,966 million for Q1 2026, up 8% from $1,816 million; adjusted operating margin 39.1%.
  • Diluted net income per share: $5.63 for Q1 2026 (net income attributable to Aon shareholders $1,212 million).
  • Adjusted diluted EPS: $6.48 for Q1 2026, up 14% from $5.67 in Q1 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Organic revenue growth of 5% driven by net new business and strong retention across client segments.
  • Risk Capital revenue increased 10% to $3,502 million, led by Commercial Risk Solutions and Reinsurance Solutions growth.
  • Human Capital revenue held near prior-year levels at $1,539 million with Health Solutions showing strength while Wealth Solutions experienced softness.
  • Generated significant cash flow: cash provided by operations $430 million and free cash flow $363 million for the quarter; repurchased 1.5 million shares and returned $662 million to shareholders.
  • Operational progress on Aon United and the 3x3 Plan, including $25 million net restructuring savings and continued integration/investment activity tied to NFP.

Original SEC Filing: Aon plc [ AON ] - 8-K - May. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
